faqs.question: Which Medical fields offer the best work-life balance?
faqs.answer:
Some medical field which offe best work and life balance are shown below -
Dentistry (BDS & MDS) – Fixed clinic hours, fewer emergencies.
Pharmacy (BPharm & MPharm) – Less stress compared to hospital-based jobs.
Physiotherapy (BPT & MPT) – Flexible working hours, minimal emergency calls.
Public Health & Community Medicine – Focus on preventive healthcare rather than emergency treatments.
Clinical Research & Medical Writing – Research-based work with no direct patient responsibilities.

placements.overview:
Established in 2022, Pune Healthcare Institute is an institute in Pune, Maharashtra. At the UG and Certificate levels, the institute offers 12 courses across 5 specialisations in Full Time mode. The popular Pune Healthcare Institute programs include UG Diploma, B.Voc and Certificate. Pune Healthcare Institute courses are provided in several streams including Medicine & Health Sciences and Science, etc. The institute has a total seat intake of 360 students. The fees at Pune Healthcare Institute ranges between INR 50,000 to 1,50,000 for the different courses. The institute has a well-laid infrastructure with multiple facilities.

Pune Healthcare Institute offers diverse programmes at UG and Certificate levels in Full Time mode. The offered programmes can be chosen from streams such as Medicine & Health Sciences and Science. At the UG level, candidates have the provision to opt from courses such as UG Diploma and B.Voc. Whereas, at Certificate level, Pune Healthcare Institute offers courses such as Certificate. Pune Healthcare Institute fee structure for all the courses is around INR 50,000 to 1,50,000.

fees_and_eligibility.overview: Pune Healthcare Institute offers diverse programmes at UG and Certificate levels in Full Time mode. Whereas, at Certificate level, Pune Healthcare Institute offers courses such as Certificate. Pune Healthcare Institute fee structure for all the courses is around INR 50,000 to 1,50,000.

highlights.summary:
The NEET UG 2026 registration window is now closed for admission to the MBBS, BDS, and other courses. The exam is scheduled for May 3, 2026.

A few more updates are given below:

reviews_list.batch_info: - Batch of 2026
reviews_list.review_title: A place to graduate as a great and infalliable physician.

review_content.Placements: College is provided with management and government quota seats for admission procedure. Our college provides post graduation courses too along with under graduate course of Bachelor of homoeopathic medicine and surgery (BHMS). And also provided with well equipped hospital and managing staff.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college having various labs for thorough study of each and every subject, of our curriculum. Like wise, anatomy labs with well maintained viscera and bones with other required equipments. Also provide digital classrooms with Wi-Fi favolities. And having central library which getting updated with new publications and editions.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Homeopathic Medicine and Surgery (BHMS) - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: The college is sooo good .

review_content.Placements: I have scored about 89% and the lowest package is 6 lap and the highest one is 42 lap they are so many companies for recruiting about 75% of our campus students got internship from our course in some companies like whipro they are so many roles offered in our course .
review_content.Infrastructure: The facilities and infrastructure is good and they are soo many facilities like there will be Wi-Fi every so you can study and work without any network issues and coming to the library they are plenty of books which you can refer easily and helps you .

reviews_list.batch_info: MD in Homoeopathy (Organon of Medicine) - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: Absolutely dissatisfied because of environment, good placement in 2023, facility is good.

review_content.Placements: Our college have 70 persent students got internship with minimum 2 LPA package with it companies. 2 3 company visit per month and got placement in high standards companies visit our college for placement and take interview and gave job to students for company.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our hostel is good quality food and master of safe cooking in centine and our college have big library for study and give environment for students for exam preparation with classroom and labs with Wi-Fi facility. Centine mentinand highenik for students.

review_content.Placements: This is an MBBS college, so I don't think there will be any placements happening here. If you consider an internship as a placement, then there will be an internship for a year at the end of the course after studying for 4.5 years. So I don't know why there is a tab for placement here in the MBBS college section.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure here is good. The hostel isn't that bad, but if you are thinking that you will get rooms like those in hotels, then you are living in a very bad delusion. Hostel rooms are spacious enough for 2. We get 2 beds, cupboards, an almirah, chairs, and tables for one room. There are AC rooms and non-AC rooms as well, though it depends on your budget which one you would like.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2023
reviews_list.review_title: Our college provided great faculty members for 2018 batch.

review_content.Placements: It is an MBBS degree so there are no placements given to students. After MBBS, every doctor can practice either in their clinic or a private hospital or can even apply for government jobs. In private hospitals, MBBS doctor receives a minimum salary of Rs.50,000 per month, and the maximum salary offered is Rs.100000 per month. In government jobs, the average salary package offered ranges between 1 LPA - 2 LPA. After MBBS, you can give NEET PG and enroll for a degree of MS or MD, and after specialisation, doctors are highly paid. Even after post-graduation, you can do DM i.e., super specialisation. The average salary package offered is 32 LPA. Top roles in MBBS are offered in clinical branches.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2025
reviews_list.review_title: Infrastructure on construction, good for academics!

review_content.Placements: There are already many vacancies for doctors. No medical college provides you with placements. But surely, you will be getting the job once you leave due to the scarcity of doctors. You will be getting at least INR 60,000 per month for six hours job on working days.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is right now under construction, as it is newly opened. Currently there are three lecture theaters in college and one lecture theater in hospital. College have separate demonstrate rooms and labs including CAL Lab for each department. There is a Central library and Outside Reading are too. GYM is available too.

highlights.summary:

Here are some additional updates regarding the C.U. Shah Medical College:

faqs.answer: A:    C.U. Shah Medical College is a good institution for PG Diploma, MD, UG Diploma, MS, MBBS courses, and these programs are delivered by highly experienced faculty. Major specialisation at C.U. Shah Medical College are Dermatology, Radiology, Radiotherapy Technology, Anaesthesia, Anatomy, Community Medicine, General Medicine, General Surgery, Microbiology, Obstetrics & Gynecology, Ophthalmology, Orthopaedics, ENT, Pediatrics, Pathology, Psychiatry.
faqs.question: Are there any specializations offered by C.U. Shah Medical College?

review_content.General: Around 90-95% of the students get jobs in government hospitals or other private hospitals and laboratories. The salary package offered ranges from 4 LPA - 6 LPA. Shall Hospital, Civil Hospital, etc., are offering jobs in our hospital. Roles offered in our course are testing blood samples, and give proper reports related to the health of patients and give them medicine and suggestions.
review_content.Infrastructure: In our college, Wi-Fi is available in every building and every classroom. There are very smart and new technology devices available for our study purpose. The library is also available in our college for reading and gets different books for study. In our college, we also have a cricket group, football ground for playing in free time. Hostel students have good canteen facilities.

reviews_list.batch_info: Post Graduate Diploma in Radio-Diagnosis - Batch of 2018
reviews_list.review_title: We have good infrastructure, good facilities in our college, but placements are not up to the mark

review_content.Placements: Our college has recently been a University, so the placements are not pretty much highlighted. Still, this year, there is news of campus placements. Last year, there weren't any campus placements organised by the college.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college is situated in the outskirts of the town. The college surroundings are good. We have good infrastructure in our college. In our college, we have the facilities like Wi-Fi, labs for every department with good facilities, classrooms with monitor and projector facilitated for the educational presentations. We have good sports activities and a ground facility for practice. We have the hostel facility with AC and non-AC rooms. The mess facility is okay.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2027
reviews_list.review_title: I am dissatisfied with the faculty members, staff members, and people around the college.

review_content.Placements: In MBBS courses at medical colleges in Gujarat, including CU Shah Medical College, placement generally takes place through internships, residence, and later employment in hospitals and healthcare institutions. The placement process often starts with the mandatory internship period, which typically lasts one year. During the internship, students get placed in various hospitals and healthcare facilities, allowing them to gain practical experience under the supervision of senior doctors. Internships often serve as a stepping stone for permanent placements in hospitals, clinics, or even research organisations.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: One of the good colleges in Gujarat.

content.Placements: I'm studying MBBS right now as S-batch in c. u. shah Medical college and hospital, surendranagar. So there is no placement like scenario in MBBS like in other courses but surely we get government job after completing our 4.5+1 year cource or else can get admission in PG (residency) and also after that u can get government job for sure. And that 1 year is for internship is compulsory for all students. There are also so many students who come from abroad after doing MBBS to do internship.
content.Infrastructure: The MBBS course is same for all colleges and universities but the way that they teach us is quite good. There are so many facilities provided by college. All departments are so humble and very responsive to students, they solve their queries, suggest to do like that, etc. There is library with good environment and also with Wi-Fi and can say that one of the best library in Gujarat. The hostel is good, there are different for both boys and girls. There are canteen, indoor games room.

individual_reviews.course: Post Graduate Diploma in Dermatology, Venereology and Leprosy - Batch of 2020
individual_reviews.title: I'm satisfied with our college because they provide every required facility.

content.Placements: Around 90-95% of the students get jobs in government hospitals or other private hospitals and laboratories. The salary package offered ranges from 4 LPA - 6 LPA. Shall Hospital, Civil Hospital, etc., are offering jobs in our hospital. Roles offered in our course are testing blood samples, and give proper reports related to the health of patients and give them medicine and suggestions.
content.Infrastructure: In our college, Wi-Fi is available in every building and every classroom. There are very smart and new technology devices available for our study purpose. The library is also available in our college for reading and gets different books for study. In our college, we also have a cricket group, football ground for playing in free time. Hostel students have good canteen facilities.

individual_reviews.course: Post Graduate Diploma in Radio-Diagnosis - Batch of 2018
individual_reviews.title: We have good infrastructure, good facilities in our college, but placements are not up to the mark

content.Placements: Our college has recently been a University, so the placements are not pretty much highlighted. Still, this year, there is news of campus placements. Last year, there weren't any campus placements organised by the college.
content.Infrastructure: The college is situated in the outskirts of the town. The college surroundings are good. We have good infrastructure in our college. In our college, we have the facilities like Wi-Fi, labs for every department with good facilities, classrooms with monitor and projector facilitated for the educational presentations. We have good sports activities and a ground facility for practice. We have the hostel facility with AC and non-AC rooms. The mess facility is okay.

individual_reviews.title: I am dissatisfied with the faculty members, staff members, and people around the college.

content.Placements: In MBBS courses at medical colleges in Gujarat, including CU Shah Medical College, placement generally takes place through internships, residence, and later employment in hospitals and healthcare institutions. The placement process often starts with the mandatory internship period, which typically lasts one year. During the internship, students get placed in various hospitals and healthcare facilities, allowing them to gain practical experience under the supervision of senior doctors. Internships often serve as a stepping stone for permanent placements in hospitals, clinics, or even research organisations.

reviews.review_heading: The great chapters of Amaltas.
reviews.review_text: I'm a day scholar hence I knew less about the hostel facilities. Overall grading of the hostel is fine but there is Wi-Fi and network issues. The mess services are average. How it will be called a mess if it does not provide 5-star services. Classrooms are air-conditioned. For sports, there are different courts.

reviews.review_heading: MBBS: A private college life.
reviews.review_text: Facilities and infrastructure of the college are well-developed. We have 2 girls' hostels and 2 boys' hostels along with a seperate college building and hospital building. Hostels are well-furnished and have 2 seater rooms available. Each room is well-spaced along with washrooms.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Amaltas Institute of Medical Science.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of our college is average, and it is not so well-developed. There is no Wi-Fi available for students either in college or in the hostel. The network is very bad. Labs are good with proper equipment. Classrooms are good too. The mess food is not great and there is no canteen in college. Medical facilities are great. It has good sports and games.

reviews.review_heading: It provides the best noble professional doctors.
reviews.review_text: There are many departments for study on the campus. You can study according to your need. There are 3 main subjects in the 1st year in anatomy, physiology, and biochemistry. All facilities are available in our college. The food served is good. It has good infrastructure and all sports facilities.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Amaltas Institute of Medical Science.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of our college is average. The campus has basic facilities, and infrastructure is also normal. The campus does not have an auditorium or a canteen. Almost all facilities are provided here. Transport facilities are also good. Bus facilities to all cities nearby are provided for free for students. Overall, it is in average condition. Our college is still developing. Further development will be seen in later years. The food provided in a mess is average. Hygiene is maintained. St...

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied with faculty and management. Teachers and staff are very cooperative.
reviews.review_text: Excellent college with the best teachers for guidance and the best infrastructure. Faculty offices and administrative units of these buildings may include lecture halls. Teachers provide support and special infrastructure. The names of classrooms, library, Wi-Fi, quality of food, canteen, and medical facilities are also good.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Amaltas Institute of Medical Science.
reviews.review_text: The sports area is not available in our college. Students have made up their areas to play in according to their comfort. An auditorium is not available. Hostel rooms are good compared to other colleges' rooms. The food in the mess is good. Most of the buildings are developed.

reviews.review_heading: It gives you good academics and a happy college life.
reviews.review_text: Our college is well facilitated by a large lecture theater with a seating capacity of 300 students, these are 5 in number. There are 15 departments undertaken in this course, which are well developed. We have a library of 500 seats with Wi-Fi and a computer lab. Hostels are well-equipped and have all the basic facilities of water, electricity, etc. Quality of food is average to be honest, but there are many options available for food in the form of canteens. 2 basketball courts, 1 football groun...

review_content.Placements: In the course, we have fifty members yearly; the highest package is 8 Lacs, and the lowest is 5 Lacs per annum. We may set up a new hospital and join the private or government hospitals and do the jobs. Everyone should have six months of internship after the course.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is poor in our college, but the faculty and the complex block are well-furnished. We have many classrooms and a library and the labs for every subject to prepare well hostel is perfect. A washing machine facility and AC are available we don't have a canteen, but the medical facilities are good, and we have many sports and games.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor Of Physiotherapy - Batch of 2020
reviews_list.review_title: The main highlight would be the teaching, and the care taken by the staff is highly inspirable.

review_content.Placements: There were no placements in our college. The students should complete their internship in our Apollo hospitals itself. If they like us, they will place in Apollo hospital, but at very low salaries. There is no need for any of the highest percentage for our field. Self-talent and the knowledge of treatment is the main key. The highest salaries offered in our field is one's own ability to treat the number of patients. Many conference programs are being conducted at our institute. We are provided with valuable international certificates.
review_content.Infrastructure: There will be no Wi-Fi for the students and the classrooms. The sports hours are given once in a week. There are no hostels for the boys provided by the college, and only a girls hostel is provided which is reasonable for the price of Rs.5,000 per month. There are no other issues during the tenure of the college.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor Of Physiotherapy - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: It is a good college with a good infrastructure.

review_content.Placements: Almost 95% of the students from our course got placed. The salary offered is Rs. 25,000 per month. A top recruiting company that visits our campus for placements is Apollo. Almost 90% of the students from our course got an internship. A top role offered is a physiotherapist. It is a good college, but it is very tough to clear subjects.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college provides good infrastructure and facilities. It provides a hostel and library facilities. The hostel facility is only for girls and not for boys. A Wi-Fi facility is available in the library. Labs have a good infrastructure. The food served in the hostel is good and tasty. I like the food in the hostel.

answer.text: Some medical field which offe best work and life balance are shown below -Dentistry (BDS & MDS) – Fixed clinic hours, fewer emergencies.Pharmacy (BPharm & MPharm) – Less stress compared to hospital-based jobs.Physiotherapy (BPT & MPT) – Flexible working hours, minimal emergency calls.Public Health & Community Medicine – Focus on preventive healthcare rather than emergency treatments.Clinical Research & Medical Writing – Research-based work with no direct patient responsibilities.

lists: Dentistry (BDS & MDS) – Fixed clinic hours, fewer emergencies.
Pharmacy (BPharm & MPharm) – Less stress compared to hospital-based jobs.
Physiotherapy (BPT & MPT) – Flexible working hours, minimal emergency calls.
Public Health & Community Medicine – Focus on preventive healthcare rather than emergency treatments.
Clinical Research & Medical Writing – Research-based work with no direct patient responsibilities.

highlights.summary:
The NEET UG 2026 registration window is now closed for admission to the MBBS, BDS, and other courses. The exam is scheduled for May 3, 2026.

Here are some additional updates regarding the K A P Viswanathan Government Medical College:

faqs.answer: A:    K A P Viswanathan Government Medical College is a well-known institution for MD, MS, UG Diploma, DM, M.Ch, MBBS courses, and these programs are delivered by highly experienced faculty. K A P Viswanathan Government Medical College has course specializations in Anaesthesia, Biochemistry, General Medicine, Microbiology, Pediatrics, Pathology, Psychiatry, Anatomy, Obstetrics & Gynecology, Ophthalmology, General Surgery, Orthopaedics, ENT, Dermatology, Toxicology, Emergency Medicine, General Nursing & Midwifery, Cardiology, Neurology.
faqs.question: What all specializations are provided by the courses offered at K A P Viswanathan Government Medical College?

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2024
reviews_list.review_title: I am impressed with its infrastructure.

review_content.Placements: Our college provides MBBS seats for students who scored sufficient marks in NEET through Tamilnadu MGR University Counselling and All India Counselling. Our college has about 150 MBBS seats. All students get an internship opportunity at the end of the course.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college has a big library, which is the best place to study during the free time. Our college has a basketball ground. Our college mess provides food based on the menu prepared by students. Our college has neat and student-friendly facilities.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: Great clinical exposure. Great faculty members.

review_content.Placements: Placements in medicine are a joke. It's all up to the efforts we put in after or during MBBS so that we can crack further exams and continue the journey to MD, MS, etc. After MBBS, one could find opportunities, but as we see, the patients are more inclined to visit specialists than MBBS degree holders. If the skills are exemplary, then one could start a clinic.
review_content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi may or may not be present at hostels. Classrooms are ok, and the library is well-equipped. Food is ok, and the canteen has snacks. Ground is present for sports. Medical facilities will be present as the government hospital is somewhat near. Basic facilities will be present.

overview_faqs.answer: K A P Viswanathan Government Medical College is a renowned institution offering MD, MS, UG Diploma, DM, M.Ch, MBBS. Students can choose these courses to embark on their careers in the field of Medicine & Health Sciences, Nursing. The institute is staffed with highly skilled and experienced faculty members dedicated to delivering these programs. K A P Viswanathan Government Medical College boasts an impressive review rating of 4.7 based on student feedback at Shiksha. Additionally, it has received a rating of 5 for Campus Life, 4.5 for Placements, 4.5 for Faculty, and 4.5 for Infrastructure.

review_summary.college_name: K A P Viswanathan Government Medical College Reviews on Placements, Faculty & Facilities

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2026
individual_reviews.title: A good college aimed at all around development along with studies.

content.Placements: About 99% students got placement at the end of the course. The package varies according to the hospital they work. Many hospitals are recruiting the students based on their talents. All the students have to undergo Compulsory Rotatory Residential Internship for a year.
content.Infrastructure: Everything is in a good condition. Library is well stocked with many books from different genres. Laboratories are well-equipped with instruments in good working condition. Classrooms are well ventilated. Food in hostel is good. Canteen is also available.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2024
individual_reviews.title: I am impressed with its infrastructure.

content.Placements: Our college provides MBBS seats for students who scored sufficient marks in NEET through Tamilnadu MGR University Counselling and All India Counselling. Our college has about 150 MBBS seats. All students get an internship opportunity at the end of the course.
content.Infrastructure: Our college has a big library, which is the best place to study during the free time. Our college has a basketball ground. Our college mess provides food based on the menu prepared by students. Our college has neat and student-friendly facilities.

individual_reviews.course: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2028
individual_reviews.title: Great clinical exposure. Great faculty members.

content.Placements: Placements in medicine are a joke. It's all up to the efforts we put in after or during MBBS so that we can crack further exams and continue the journey to MD, MS, etc. After MBBS, one could find opportunities, but as we see, the patients are more inclined to visit specialists than MBBS degree holders. If the skills are exemplary, then one could start a clinic.
content.Infrastructure: Wi-Fi may or may not be present at hostels. Classrooms are ok, and the library is well-equipped. Food is ok, and the canteen has snacks. Ground is present for sports. Medical facilities will be present as the government hospital is somewhat near. Basic facilities will be present.

individual_reviews.course: DM in Cardiology - Batch of 2022
individual_reviews.title: Almost everything about it is good.

content.Placements: I have a Diploma in Laboratory Management, and now I am working in a Government mission hospital, earning almost 8 LPA. No top recruiting companies come to select students because it's a course program, and companies do not typically recruit for such roles. Roles are in laboratory management, etc.
content.Infrastructure: Infrastructure provided, like Wi-Fi, classrooms, and the library, are all very good and well-maintained. Wi-Fi speed is also good, and classrooms have fully fitted air conditioning. The food in the canteen is good, and there are many food options for the students that they want to eat. No sports and games are provided.

college_info.intro: Cheran College of Pharmacy offers Undergraduate, Postgraduate and PhD courses. The flagship course at Cheran College of Pharmacy is BPharm. It is a four-year duration undergraduate programme that is divided into eight semesters. Candidates need to fulfil the eligibility criteria, that is, a candidate must have passed Class 12 with a minimum of 50% marks aggregate in Physics, Chemistry with Biology /Mathematics/Computer science. A pass in the Diploma in Pharmacy is also eligible for direct entry into the second year of BPharm course. Candidates can refer to the information below to know more about Cheran College of Pharmacy popular programmes:

It concentrates on the holistic skill development of the student that includes communication, technical, aptitude and skills. The cell conducts skill-based assessments to evaluate the performance of the student. The students exposed to the latest trends and techniques, which enable them to be acknowledged by the industry.

faqs.answer: A:    The fee bracket for programs offerred by the Mandya Institute of Medical Sciences, Mandya is approximately INR 117450 - INR 3495497.

reviews_list.review_title: It is one of the best medical colleges in Karnataka, MIMS, Mandya.

review_content.Placements: MIMS is a government institution for medical students. It has 4 years of education and one year of internship in the same government hospital with a monthly salary. After that, students usually take another NEET PG exam to choose their post-graduation courses.
review_content.Infrastructure: The main college campus is one single building with 6 floors, including the basement, with a terrace above the 4th floor. It has the first herbal and terrace garden in Karnataka. All the classrooms are well-equipped with projectors and mics. The hostel is average, each room accommodates 3 people with a separate cot and table, a wardrobe, and an attached bathroom. Mess food is good, the menu doesn't change much. Extracurricular activities are well supported.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2027
reviews_list.review_title: Everything about Mandya Medical College.

review_content.Placements: I'm studying MBBS in a medical college in Mandya. You have to do an internship after the four years of the course, during which you will be paid 30,000 as a stipend. After that, you will receive your degree or license as a legal medical practitioner, allowing you to join any hospital as a doctor or open your own clinic and earn a minimum of 70,000 based on your capacity and knowledge of the subject.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our college follows a modern education method where teachers prepare their own PPTs, display them, and explain with the help of the slides. Since the class size is around 150, you might not be able to pay proper attention. The hostel facilities are kind of okay, but not that good.

reviews_list.batch_info: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: A quick review of the state medical college

review_content.Placements: I'm now studying in the first year of MBBS, and about 150 students are allotted seats every year here. At the end of the MBBS course, we need to complete a one-month internship, which provides a salary of about 30,000. We must clear the university examinations to be eligible for the internship.
review_content.Infrastructure: The infrastructure is good but old, including the hostel. We are given three-sharing rooms with all necessary facilities, including a table, chair, and cupboard. The library, classrooms, and laboratories are well-maintained. There is also a ground on the college campus.

reviews.review_heading: My extraordinary college MIMS.
reviews.review_text: As my college is a government-run college, infrastructure of college is well-maintained. Our college has a central library which has all the features including the digital library. Our campus also has facilities of hostel for both boys and girls which is well-maintained.

reviews.review_heading: The real fate of the government medical college in Karnataka.
reviews.review_text: Our college has a hostel, but it is not good. Our college has moderate classrooms, and not high-level classrooms. Our college has a good central library where we can go and study. Free Wi-Fi is available in the library.

reviews.review_heading: It is one of the best medical colleges in Karnataka, MIMS, Mandya.
reviews.review_text: The main college campus is one single building with 6 floors, including the basement, with a terrace above the 4th floor. It has the first herbal and terrace garden in Karnataka. All the classrooms are well-equipped with projectors and mics. The hostel is average, each room accommodates 3 people with a separate cot and table, a wardrobe, and an attached bathroom. Mess food is good, the menu doesn't change much. Extracurricular activities are well supported.

reviews.review_heading: Everything about Mandya Medical College.
reviews.review_text: Our college follows a modern education method where teachers prepare their own PPTs, display them, and explain with the help of the slides. Since the class size is around 150, you might not be able to pay proper attention. The hostel facilities are kind of okay, but not that good.

reviews.review_heading: A quick review of the state medical college
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure is good but old, including the hostel. We are given three-sharing rooms with all necessary facilities, including a table, chair, and cupboard. The library, classrooms, and laboratories are well-maintained. There is also a ground on the college campus.

reviews.review_heading: MIMS is very good for learning, apart from hostel and mess.
reviews.review_text: There is a separate library and reading room in college with Wi-Fi. Very spacious classrooms. Good equipment in the lab. Food quality is not at all good, and that is unhygienic. Menu will be repeated weakly once, hostel! Maintenance fee is 25300 and monthly Mess bill is around 3500-4000. Should be paid, which is unworthy compared to the PG that is nearby to us.

reviews.review_heading: College is absolutely fine, but the hostel is not at all good.
reviews.review_text: There is a library and reading room in college itself. Very huge classrooms, and there is Wi-Fi in the library and reading room. The hostel rooms are not well maintained, and the mess is very ugly. The food that they provide is not at all good and unhygienic.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Mandya Institute of Medical Sciences, Mandya.
reviews.review_text: Our college facilities and infrastructure are pretty good, so we can continue our course peacefully for 5.5 years. Our labs are very clean and well maintained by workers. The library is situated in the college itself and opened throughout the day. And the quality of the food here is pretty nice and hygienic.

reviews.review_heading: Review of Mandya Institute of Medical Sciences, Mandya.
reviews.review_text: The infrastructure of the college is minimal, and the labs are well designed with sophisticated and well-equipped instruments. Classrooms are well-equipped with modern learning facilities, and the library has highly professional magazines and books with the availability of a 24x7 Wi-Fi facility.

reviews.review_heading: This is about my college life at Mandya Institute of Medical Sciences, Mandya.
reviews.review_text: The college infrastructure is good, but the hostel infrastructure is very bad. Hot water is not available 24*7, and the mess is available but the food is not up to the mark. Hygiene maintenance is poor, and free Wi-Fi is not available so we have to pay for a connection. There are three shared B.Ed rooms available.

reviews.review_heading: Worth choosing, read more .
reviews.review_text: South Karnataka people will feel it okay for the mess food, however it will be good. Don't have high expectations by watching shots; the mess is run by students itself, but the infrastructure of the hostel is not up to its marks and name (it was built around 2006, I guess); the college is good; there is no gym; the library has a Wi-Fi connection; the classroom could get good ( it's not good at all); you will have a good time in sports here; everyone is competitive; we have good ground .

reviews.review_heading: Satisfied with the faculty and satisfied with the maintenance.
reviews.review_text: Average maintenance in hostels, and in college washrooms, broken fans, mess food taste-wise good, but very unhealthy; the condition of rooms is from average to bad; some rooms do not have fans; overall, the condition inside the college is above average while in the hostel it is below average.

reviews.review_heading: Our college is a very good college.
reviews.review_text: The college has a library, Wi-Fi, and a recently started cafeteria for students and staff members. The hostel is average. Our college and hostel facilities are average.

reviews.review_heading: The best government medical college in Karnataka. It has an excellent campus.
reviews.review_text: In our college, the classrooms and library are awesome. We have a digital library. Wi-Fi is available in the library with an air-conditioning facility. Labs and classrooms are the best. The hostel is average, but it's good compared to other government medical colleges. Food is generally healthy.

reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfied and a proud student.
reviews.review_text: The college has a huge library along with Wi-Fi. The college campus is also pretty huge and also has huge laboratories and demonstration rooms. They are also equipped with quality instruments for teaching. The library also has attached study halls, there has also been preparations to set up E-library.

highlights.summary: The NEET UG 2026 registration window is now closed for admission to the MBBS, BDS, and other courses. The exam is scheduled for May 3, 2026.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2029
reviews_list.review_title: Overall good college where you come just for studies.

review_content.Placements: It's a new college and the first internship started this year and going well. The stipend is Rs. 13,900 only as such no placements are offered at this moment. College started its first batch in 2020, so there is a first batch doing internships here. Management is better not best.
review_content.Infrastructure: College building is under construction. Hostels are good better than some of the government colleges. Hospital patient loads are average. Library for self-study which is open from 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM. Wi-Fi facility is available. The quality of food is not good in the hostel but the rooms are better and well-maintained. Labs have the proper equipment. There is a cafe and a canteen in the college.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MBBS) - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: Review of Mahaveer Institute of Medical Sciences and Research.

review_content.Placements: I am in 2nd year. There are only 4 batches that have been admitted to the college. The first batch of 2020 is going to do internships this year and in a month they will start the internship.
review_content.Infrastructure: Hostels are good and maintained. It has 2 seater rooms with attached bathrooms. The college is still under construction, and the whole thing is not built yet. The hostel is also under construction. The campus does not have an auditorium or gym. For sports, there is no ground.
review_content.Faculty: The college is academic-oriented only. They are strict with their studies and want the focus to remain on academics only. Teachers pay attention to performance in college exams and tests. They are very strict with attendance and 75% is a must to appear in final exams. The NMC-guided course curriculum is followed in the college and they teach accordingly.

highlights.summary: Swami Vivekananda National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research admission 2025 is open. Candidates can visit the official website of the college.

college_info.intro:
SVNIRTAR offers 4 UG Courses and 3 PG Courses across Medicine & Health Sciences or Nursing streams. All courses at SVNIRTAR are full-time offline-mode only with the most popular courses being Bachelors in Occupational Therapy and Bachelors in Physiotherapy among UG Courses and Ortho/Rehabilitation Nsg among PG Courses.
SVNIRTAR, Cuttack also offers various specialisations such as Rehabilitation, Musculoskeletal Conditions, Paediatrics & Neurology for MPT, and Rehabilitation, Hand Rehabilitation, Developmental Disabilities & Neurology for MOT. Alongside these courses, they also offer various diplomas and additional short-term courses and certifications such as Diploma in Sign Language Interpretation, Certificate in Bench Skill, etc. Candidates can check the SVNIRTAR popular programmes in the table provided below:

faqs.answer: A:    According to Linkedin.com, a majority of alumni from SVNIRTAR end up working in the Healthcare Services sector, Education comes a close second. Furthermore, most alumni of SVNIRTAR are stated to be specialised in the fields of Rehabilitation or Occupational Therapy. Major recruiters that hire from SVNIRTAR include Otto-Bock, Endolite, ALIMCO, Astha Therapeutics Intervention Pvt. Ltd., Jindal Power and Steel Ltd.
faqs.question: Which sector has placed the most amount no. of SVNIRTAR students?

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, SVNIRTAR does offer placement support in the form of Placement Events for final year students with interviewers from both national and international companies like Otto-Bock, Endolite, ALIMCO, Astha Therapeutics Intervention Pvt. Ltd., Jindal Power and Steel Ltd. conducting campus interviews to recruit prospective students into their companies.
faqs.question: Does SVNIRTAR offer any placement supports?

faqs.answer: A:    SVNIRTAR College has a dedicated placement cell and offers good placement opportunities for students who wish to build a career in Healthcare/Rehabilitation services. SVNIRTAR, Cuttack also hosts Campus Recruitment events every year where national and international companies come to conduct interviews for Final Year students. Top recruiters for SVNIRTAR, Orissa include Otto-Bock, Endolite, ALIMCO, Astha Therapeutics Intervention Pvt. Ltd., Jindal Power and Steel Ltd.
faqs.question: How are the placements in SVNIRTAR?

faqs.answer: A:    Yes, there is a dedicated placement cell at SVNIRTAR, Cuttack that helps students get in touch with top companies in the field of Medical Technology. Not only does the placement cell host Campus Interviews with top companies, but it also helps students build their resumes offering hands-on training and 6-month-long internship opportunities. The top recruiters for SVNIRTAR, Orissa alumni include Otto-Bock, Endolite, ALIMCO, Astha Therapeutics Intervention Pvt. Ltd., Jindal Power and Steel L
faqs.question: Is there a placement cell at SVNIRTAR?

fees_and_eligibility.overview: Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research offers a wide range of programmes to students at the UG and PG levels. The college is mainly concerned with streams such as Medicine & Health Sciences and Nursing. At the UG level, the college offers courses such as B.O.Th and BPT. At the PG level, the college offers courses such as PG Diploma. Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research courses are offered to students in Full Time mode. Further, the fees structure for Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research courses stands around INR 60,270.

faqs.answer: A:    Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses: CoursesEligibilityBPTCandidate must have passed 10+2 or equivalent examination from a recognised school/board.
faqs.question: What is the eligibility to get admission in Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research for UG/PG courses?

faqs.answer: A:    No, to pursue a PG course like Masters in Physiotherapy from SVNIRTAR an applicant must have completed the following requirements:One must have completed a full-time regular BPT or BSc (PT) course at any UniversityCompleted any and all requirements of the degree courseComplete the required Internship duration of 6 months for the above course before 29-07-2025
faqs.question: Can a student apply for SVNIRTAR Masters in Physiotherapy in absence of final results of qualifying degree?

overview_faqs.question: What is SVNIRTAR famous for?
overview_faqs.answer: SVNIRTAR is famous for its specialisation in the field of Rehabilitation of persons with Locomotor Disabilities, and offers various courses related to this field such as Bachelors in Prosthetics and Orthotics. Moreover, it is not only an educational institute, but also continues to provide rehabilitation services to patients allowing students to gain hand-on experience with real patients.

overview_faqs.question: Is SVNIRTAR a good college?
overview_faqs.answer: SVNIRTAR is an excellent college for those who wish to pursue a career in the field of rehabilitation of persons with locomotor disability, SVNIRTAR, Cuttack is also uniquely specialised in this area offering hands-on experience with patients and is equipped with state of the art labs and equipment. Furthermore, students of SVNIRTAR, Orissa consistently receive placements and internships with national and international companies in the field of medical technology and prostheses manufacture.

overview_faqs.question: Is it worth to consider Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research as a good Institute?
overview_faqs.answer: Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research is a reputed institute for BPT, PG Diploma. Students can opt these programs to pursue their career in Medicine & Health Sciences, Nursing. Institute has trained & experienced faculty who deliver these programs. As per Shiksha verified reviews, Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research has a rating of 4.1 on the basis of reviews by students. Not just this, it has also been rated 3.9 on campuslife, 3.8 on placements, 4.6 on faculty and 3.9 on Infrastructure.

faqs.question: When are SVNIRTAR application forms out?
faqs.answer: SVNIRTAR applications were released on Thursday May 17,  2025 for the admission cycle of 2025 and their admission cycles usually begin in mid-May annually. To remain updated on SVNIRTAR admission cycles, head to admission.svnirtar.nic.in to periodically check for any news, updates or announcements on their bullet-in board.

faqs.question: Can I get direct admission at SVNIRTAR?
faqs.answer: No, unfortunately SVNIRTAR does not offers direct admissions and only offers admissions based on the merit-list announced after appearing for eitherCET or PGETexams forUG or PGlevel courses respectively. After the initial merit-list is generated, candidates can select a list of preferred Specialty/Choice of Institute and depending on their rank on the merit-list and the competition for that area they will be allocated a seat.

faqs.question: Is the application fees refundable at SVNIRTAR?
faqs.answer: No, application fees are not refundable at SVNIRTAR. According to official statements made on the SVNIRTAR information brochure, under no circumstances are any application fees refundable once the payment has been made.  Moreover, other non-refundable deposits includeadmission feesafterprovisional admission. Safety or Security deposits however, arepartially refundableafter deduction oflosses/damages.

I heard from hostel students in my classroom that there were many games and sports, annual day celebrations, fests, and many other activities in the college.

paragraphs: BDU admission processstarts with registration for the preferred course. Candidates must fulfil the eligibility requirements to apply for admission. The selection for most of the programmes is entrance-based. Below is step-wise admission process for Bharathidasan University:
STEP 1: BDU Application Process 2025

Candidates can collect the prospectus and the application form from the university campus or apply online. Aspirants applying for CDOE courses have to pay an application fee of INR 250 for UG courses, INR 400 for PG and INR 550 for MBA/ MCA. The application fee for PG extension centre courses is INR 500.
STEP 2: Selection

Bharathidasan University selects candidates for most of the UG and PG courses based on merit in the last qualifying exam. For MBA, the university offers admission through TANCET score. CEETA PG score is considered for admission to MTech course of BDU. Besides, PhD admission is also entrance exam. Aspirants selected for PhD are also required to come for an interview round.
STEP 3: Counselling

Once the candidates are selected, they are called for counselling. For MBA and MTech,TANCET CounsellingandCEETA PG Counsellingare done. Aspirants attending university counselling must bring the specified documents for verification. Shortlisted candidates need to pay course fee to confirm their seat in the university.

Bharathidasan University fee is a sum of multiple components, such as tuition fee, hostel fee, library deposit, training and exam fee, etc. There are a few components that are one-time payable, while some have to be paid semester-wise or annually, such as tuition fees. faqs.question: Which entrance exam is accepted by Bharathidasan University?
faqs.answer: Yes, Bharathidasan University accepts admission to MBA course either through state-levelTANCETexam followed by TANCET Counselling. The university offers admission to MTech course based onCEETA PGscore. Besides, the university conducts an entrance exam for Integrated BTech + MTech and PhD courses. The entrance test will based on the basic knowledge of English, General Aptitude, Intelligence, Reasoning and Computer/ Internet awareness of a candidate in addition to the respective branch domain in the MTech.

faqs.question: What is the last date for Bharathidasan University distance education admission?
faqs.answer: Bharathidasan University Distance Education admissions will open online. Till now, no schedule has been released by the university. Once the application portal is live, candidates can apply online for their preferred course. CODE offers UG and PG courses such as BA, BSc, BCom, MA, MSc, etc. in online or distance education mode. The admission to most of the courses is based on merit in the last qualifying exam.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility for PhD at Bharathidasan University?
faqs.answer: At Bharathidasan University, the eligibility for PhD is to post-graduate with 50% aggregate from a recognised University (for candidates belonging to 50% SC/ST).

faqs.question: How are the placements at Bharathidasan University?
faqs.answer: Bharathidasan University offers a decent placement rate, with students from almost all the courses getting placement assistance. During Bharathidasan University placements 2023, a total of 59 UG 3-years and 318 PG 2-years students were placed in reputed companies. Further, the median package offered to UG 3-year and PG 2-year students during Bharathidasan University placements 2023 was INR 1.80 LPA and INR 2.16 LPA, respectively. The key highlights of Bharathidasan University placements for the Class of 2022 and 2023 in the table below: Particulars Placement Statistics (2022) Placement Statistics (2023) Median package (UG 3-years) INR 1.56 LPA INR 1.80 LPA Total students (UG 3-years) 93 94 Total students placed (UG 3-years) 31 59 Median package (PG 2-years) INR 2.40 LPA INR 2.16 LPA Total students (PG 2-years) 577 800 Total students placed (PG 2-years) 235 318 Note : The above-mentioned data are gathered from the Bharathidasan University NIRF report 2024.

highlights.description: Bharathidasan University is one of the most popular institutes of the country. Most of the ranking bodies have recognised this institute as one of the popular choices among students. The institute has been ranked #61 by the NIRF 2025 under the category Overall, #36 under University, and #16 under State Public University. The institute has been ranked well by eminent ranking bodies because of its curriculum, infrastructure and well-qualified faculty. Students can check the article in this space for more details around the Bharathidasan University rankings.

rankings.highlight: 9th Best Among NIRF Ranked Colleges In Tamil Nadu In 2025
5th Best Among NIRF Ranked Colleges In Tamil Nadu In 2025

nirf.description:
According to the rankings from NIRF, Bharathidasan University outperformed various other institutes, securing a higher position for its achievements in Teaching, Graduation Success Rates, Research, and creating an inclusive, quality learning environment. Bharathidasan University's rankings in 'Overall' category have shown a fluctuating trend as per the rankings of the previous years. In 2025, Bharathidasan University fell 6 ranks to #61 rank in the 'Overall' category by the NIRF ranking.
The ranks granted to the institute in the 'University' category have shown a fluctuating trend when compared to last year's rankings. In 2025, Bharathidasan University's rank is #36, the same as last year's rank in the 'University' category by the NIRF ranking.
The rankings of the institute in the 'State Public University' category have displayed a stable trend when evaluated against previous year's ranking. In 2025, Bharathidasan University's rank is #16, the same as last year's rank in the 'State Public University' category by the NIRF ranking.
Check out the updated rankings and category details from different ranking bodies below.

gallery_page.college_name: Bharathidasan University Photos & Videos

hostel.description: Bharathidasan University's hostel has a total of 1460 students staying in the hostels. Among them 694 are boys and 766 are girls. There are separately integrated dining halls for boys and girls. The hostel rooms are provided with fans, good lighting, adequate water facility, recreation centre, playing ground, newspapers and magazines. Each hostel is provided with a television and cable connectivity. Similarly, all the hostels have Wi-Fi facility. The hostels are provided with a good hygienic cooking facility and spacious dining halls. There is 24 hours watch and ward for women’s hostels. Each hostel is provided with hot water facility in the bathrooms. The hostels have grievance redressal Committee and purchase committee which meet once in a month. The hostels are provided with a telephone facility to receive calls from outside.

library.description: Bharathidasan University's library is well furnished with all resources including e-library resources. The Library has separate halls for housing the books, journals, magazines and reference books in addition to a big and well-furnished reading hall. In terms of learning resources, the library has a good collection of books and e-books in addition to subscribing a number of journals and audio-video cassettes. The library has INFLIBNET and DELNET facilities. The UGC Infonet facility helps to access more than 10000 e-resources including thousands of e-journals. Through the university's digital library, the students and faculty members can have access to more than 5000 back volumes and 7000 electronic books.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Swami Dayananda College of Arts and ScienceReviewed on 8 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Really good infrastructure and good environment surrounded by green fields.
reviews.review_text: Basically, the hostel is very good. It has good facilities and a good atmosphere, surrounded by a green environment. Our college hostel guardian is responsible and friendly, which is a plus point. We have good food, a good place to sleep, a good computer lab, a communication lab, and the infrastructure is very good. We also have a multifunctional library.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2020Offered by Dr. N.G.P. Arts and Science CollegeReviewed on 27 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: It is the best college in Coimbatore.
reviews.review_text: The college had a library, labs, and good classrooms. I was not a hostel student. I was a day scholar, and it was awesome. I heard from hostel students in my classroom that there were many games and sports, annual day celebrations, fests, and many other activities in the college.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by Dr. N.G.P. Arts and Science CollegeReviewed on 11 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: I am highly satisfied with the college.
reviews.review_text: The college provides proper and hygienic hostels. It has a good library, and a digital library is also available. In the case of facilities, everything is top-notch as compared to other colleges around Coimbatore. It also provides free Wi-Fi and digital libraries free of cost.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2023Offered by St.Joseph's College, TiruchirappalliReviewed on 6 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Good college for MCA course.
reviews.review_text: More than four computer laboratories, two canteens, and historic buildings are among the many facilities. The canteens’ food is of high quality. Scholarships are available for kids from low-income families, a green and clean environment is maintained, and a free midday meal programme is provided for those in need.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Bishop Heber CollegeReviewed on 17 Oct 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Review of Bishop Heber College, Social Work Department.
reviews.review_text: The classrooms and hostels are average compared to other colleges. The library is very good with an e-library. There is a very good canteen and sports facilities. You can even get a sports scholarship. Free Wi-Fi is available if you have a laptop. There are very good computer labs and other labs, such as a botany lab and labs for visual communication.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Dr. N.G.P. Arts and Science CollegeReviewed on 13 Oct 2025)
reviews.review_heading: College is 7 out of 10, but everything has its own pros and cons, so you’ve to learn how to survive.
reviews.review_text: Our college has a lot of facilities. We can have the respected Wi-Fi, labs, and library time. Recently, our college time has changed, and we are getting a lot of time to explore our campus. The food is great, and we get equal and tasty food on campus at a cheap rate.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by St.Joseph's College, TiruchirappalliReviewed on 26 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: 180 years old and a heritage college in Tamil Nadu.
reviews.review_text: All kinds of facilities include more than 4 computer labs, 2 canteens, and old and heritage buildings. The quality of food in the canteen is good. Free mid day meals scheme for the financially backward class, Scholarships for financially backward students, and a Green and fresh environment.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by St.Joseph's College, TiruchirappalliReviewed on 16 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Moderate satisfied with faculty and absolutely dissatisfied with fee structure.
reviews.review_text: In our department, classrooms are well organised and neat. Wi-Fi is not provided in our department. Library is good. Hostel facility is nice. Food quality is better but weekly two times chicken will be provided in the hostel. College provide more care for sports.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by J.J. College of Arts and ScienceReviewed on 6 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Raising their standards to higher.
reviews.review_text: Administration of the college focuses on the goals of the students, and arranges the possible things required for achieving them. Their educational standards are too good. But requires improvement by means of providing proper facilities. But it's one of the underrated colleges.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Bishop Heber CollegeReviewed on 2 Sep 2025)
reviews.review_heading: The ambiance is too good.
reviews.review_text: Free Wi-Fi is available in the whole college and hostels. It provides good communication. They are giving more importance to sports, NCC, and NSS. There are camps, and field work is more important. There are more than 30 clubs at the college. I truly recommend this college. Canteen is too good.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by St.Joseph's College, TiruchirappalliReviewed on 31 Aug 2025)
reviews.review_heading: A Heritage Campus with Strong Academics, Supportive Faculty, and Good Placements.
reviews.review_text: The college boasts the Arrupe Library housing over 160,000 volumes including rare collections and more than 200 print journals. There are also 15+ departmental libraries supporting varied faculty and student needs. A robust IT infrastructure includes the Jerome Centre for ICT multiple computer laboratories (including four dedicated labs with 190 systems at the Ehrhart Centre), 24/7 Wi-Fi, an ICT centre, and language labs. The college offers several boys' hostels (Live's House, New Hostel, Sacred...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Dhanalakshmi Srinivasan College Of Arts And Science For Women - DSCASWReviewed on 28 Aug 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Good placements, best faculty members, excellent infrastructure, etc.
reviews.review_text: We have a library, cafeteria, auditorium, spacious classrooms, playground, Wi-Fi, labs, indoor sports hall, yoga hall, and a beautiful garden. Our college provides a good hostel. The college canteen and mess provide good food. Our college has medical facilities for students and all.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by St.Joseph's College, TiruchirappalliReviewed on 23 Aug 2025)
reviews.review_heading: This is the best college for MBA course.
reviews.review_text: Our college ranks among the top institutions in the nation regarding services and facilities. It offers boundless Wi-Fi access across the campus. Its velocity is 200 Mbps. Our college offers amenities such as a library, student accommodation, and more. Our college is equipped with air conditioning. The accommodation is excellent. The student body managers the mess menu, resulting in monthly changes.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by St.Joseph's College, TiruchirappalliReviewed on 23 Aug 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Overall, the best college for M.Com. Course.
reviews.review_text: In terms of infrastructure, it is the best college. Wi-Fi is supposed to be provided throughout the campus, but it is still not available. The hostel needs to be improved considerably. Institutions around the world have not used the same hostel in the same way for the last 50 years. Rooms should be properly arranged and locked. Overall, some improvements need to be made.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2022Offered by St.Joseph's College, TiruchirappalliReviewed on 22 Aug 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Best college ever for the M.Sc. In the Chemistry course.
reviews.review_text: Our college is well-structured and is currently being created. Healthcare and athletic facilities are exceptional. You will receive 1 GB of data every day over Wi-Fi, as well as unlimited call services. Even messed-up food tastes good. There are 16 hostels, with 12 designed for boys and 4 for girls. admission_overview.college_name: BAMU Aurangabad Course Admissions 2025: Courses, Eligibility, Tuition Fees & Application Process

paragraphs: BAMU admission 2025is open. It offers various courses at the UG, PG, and diploma levels. These are offered in fields such as Arts, Law, Commerce, Science, Management and Technology. UG admissions are merit-based and PG admissions are based on the CET exam.
Admission to the LLB course atDr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada Universityis based on the MHCET Law entrance examination.

Interested aspirants seeking admission to Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University, need to meet the eligibility criteria for their desired courses. Admission to the BAMU undergraduate programme is based on the 12th examination performance. Admission to Bachelor of Legislative Law (LLB) requires a minimum 45% aggregate in intermediate. Postgraduate programmes require applicants to have passed graduation from a recognised institute or university. Explore the below details of eligibility and selection criteria for Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University:

Note: Candidates must note that this information is sourced from the official website of the institute/university-sanctioning body and is subject to change.

faqs.question: What is the admission procedure for MSc programme at BAMU Aurangabad?
faqs.answer: Admission to MSc programme at BAMU Aurangabad is done basis a Common Entrance Test (CET).

faqs.question: What is the BAMU Aurangabad MAH MBA CET Cutoff 2025?
faqs.answer: TheBAMU Aurangabad MAH MBA CET Cutoff 2025was released for admission to the MBA course for all rounds and various categories under both the All India and Home State quota. The cutoff was in the form of opening and closing percentiles.For the General AI category candidates, the first round cutoff was73,and the last round cutoff was66.49for admission to  BAMU Aurangabad. Please remember that each category, round, quota, etc., will have different cutoffs accordingly.

faqs.question: Who can apply to MA at BAMU University?
faqs.answer: To be eligible for admission to MA at BAMU University, candidates must pass graduation in the relevant stream from a recognised university. It is mandatory to meet the basic educational requirements of the course as the 50% weightage is given to past academic records in the final selection process.

faqs.question: What is the BAMU Aurangabad MAH MArch CET Cutoff 2025?
faqs.answer: TheBAMU Aurangabad MAH MArch CET Cutoff 2025was released for admission to theM.P.Ed.andM.Edcourses for all rounds and categories belonging to the All India and Home State quota candidates.For the General AI category, the cutoff in first and the only round, the cutoff varied between62.4and112.78, making theMaster of Physical Education (M.P.Ed.)the most sought-after course for admission.Please note that the cutoff for other courses, rounds, categories, and quotas will differ accordingly.

placement_data.title: BAMU Aurangabad Placement overview
paragraphs: According to BAMU Aurangabad NIRF report 2024, the median package offered during BAMU Aurangabad UG 3-year and UG 4-year placements 2023 stood at INR 1.5 LPA and INR 3.6 LPA , respectively. Further, the median package offered during BAMU Aurangabad PG 2-year placements 2023 stood at INR 2.4 LPA .
BAMU Aurangabad Placements 2023

faqs.question: How are BAMU Aurangabad placements?

faqs.question: What is the fee structure for the MBA IB programme at BAMU Aurangabad?
faqs.answer: The fee for the MBA IB programme at BAMU Aurangabad is INR 20,482.

faqs.question: What is the BAMU BCom application fees?
faqs.answer: Candidates seeking admission to the BCom programme are first required to fill out the detailed application form and pay the BAMU BCom application fees online of INR 200 for general category candidates and INR 100 for reserved category, EWS, Specially abled students and, Ex-Serviceman ward.

faqs.question: What is the fees for BA at BAMU?
faqs.answer: The fees for a three-year BA programme offered at Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University ranges between INR 642 and INR 60,000 depending upon the specialisation selected by candidates. Apart from the tuition fees, candidates might also be required to pay the hostel fees and, one-time fees during the time of admission. Note: This information is sourced from official website/ sanctioning body and is subject to change.

description: BAMU Aurangabad Cutoff 2025 has been released for all rounds and categories for admission to MCA courses based on the MAH MCA CET exam. Students can also scroll down the page to know the cutoff of various other accepting exams. A few more exam-related updates are below:

Students can refer to the table below to know the BAMU Aurangabad BTech cutoff 2025, as well as the cutoff data for 2024 and 2023. For the General AI category, the top three hardest colleges to gain admission to for the BTech CSE programme were G.S. Mandal's Marathwada Institute of Technology (MIT, Aurangabad), followed by P.E.S. College of Engineering and Deogiri Institute of Engineering and Management Studies, with the last round cutoffs of 148310, 151731 and 161959, respectively.

Now that BAMU Aurangabad admissions are over for the academic cycle 2025-26, students can refer to the table below to know the important dates related to admission to the upcoming academic year. The table below has the essential dates for exams, results, etc.

The JEE Main exam is conducted by the National Testing Agency (NTA) twice a year. It is oneof the most competitive and important entrance exams in India. Students who want to gain admission to this university's BTech course via the JEE Main exam can check out the table below to know the past years' counselling cutoffs for 2025, 2024, and 2023 for various rounds.

MHT CET is the entrance exam for candidates seeking admission to the BE/BTech and other courses. The cutoff was released across different categories under the Home State quota. For the General category, the BAMU Aurangabad B.E. / B.Tech cutoff 2025 ranged between 82.13 and 91.7, with the most sought-after branch for admission being Pharmaceutical, which had the highest cutoff percentile of 91.7. Similarly, with a lowest last-round cutoff percentile of 82.13, Food Technology became the least comp

BAMU Aurangabad MHT CET cutoff 2025 was released as percentiles, which means the higher the cutoff percentile, the more competitive the course is for admission and vice versa. Considering the last round cutoff data of 2025, Pharmaceutical turned out to be the most competitive for admission, with a cutoff closing at 91.7. For the same competitive branch, the last round cutoff was 76.72 in the General category under the HS quota.
Some of the important dates related to MHT CET 2026 for admission are provided below. Candidates can keep track of the upcoming dates to be eligible for admission.
The cutoff PDFs below can be downloaded to view the cutoff details of the last three years for multiple rounds. This will help students understand the past years' cutoff trends.

BAMU Aurangabad MHCET Law cutoff 2025 was released for admission to the BA LLB course for all rounds and categories under both the All India and Home State quota. For the General AI quota, the overall cutoff score varied between 59.03 and 86.07, wherein the hardest college for admission in the first round was Manikchand Pahade Law College, with a cutoff of 86.07.
In this section, students can refer to the table below to know the BAMU Aurangabad BA LLB cutoff 2025, as well as the cutoff for 2024 and 2023.

In the last round for the General AI students, the hardest college for admission was Manikchand Pahade Law College, with a cutoff of 59.03 in 2025. In 2024 and 2023, the cutoffs were 73.93 and 101, respectively. After analysing the scores, we can say that the competition has decreased over the years. See the table below for more information.

Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University admissions are done on the basis of MHT CET Law entrance exam for the BA LLB course. Students who are aiming for a seat at this university for the same reason can refer to the table below to know the essential timeline for admission in the upcoming academic session.

Now that admissions for the year 2025-26 are over, candidates can refer to the table below to either view or download the past years' round and year-wise counselling cutoffs for 2025, 2024 and 2023 for various categories. Analysing the cutoff for various years can help students know what the cutoff trend can look like in the subsequent admission year.

BAMU Aurangabad MAH B.Ed CET cutoff 2025 was released for admission to the Bachelor of Education course for rounds and categories. The cutoff was released in the form of opening and closing scores for both the All India and Home State quotas.

For the General AI category, the overall cutoff range varied between 14.02 and 91.49, wherein Goverment College of Education, Aurangabad was the toughest college for admission, with the last round cutoff of 91.49. On the other hand, the easiest college in

The overall BAMU Aurangabad BEd cutoff 2025 for the General HS quota fell between 93.35 and 0.21 scores. According to the last round cutoff, the most and least competitive colleges for admission were Goverment College of Education, Aurangabad and A.D. Deshmukh College of Education, with the scores of 92.16 and 0.21, respectively.

Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University accepts the MAH BEd CET entrance examination cutoff scores for admission to the BEd course. Some important dates for enrolment to the BAMU Aurangabad MAH B.Ed CET cutoff 2026 are given in the table below. Students can download these dates as a PDF file for later reference.

Getting an idea of different years’ cutoff trends helps students prepare better for upcoming examinations in the academic year 2026-27. To assist with the same, the table below provides the MAH B.Ed CET cutoff for 2025, 2024, and 2023. Students can download the year-wise PDF for future reference.

The BAMU Aurangabad MAH MBA CET cutoff 2025 is provided in the table below for MBA/PGDM admission, among other courses. The cutoff was released across different categories. For the General AI category, the last-round cutoff percentile for MBA admission was 22.6. For the same course, the cutoff stood at 73 in the first round. More details are given below:

Candidates can refer to the table below to view and compare the last three years of MAH MBA CET cutoff data for admission to the MBA course. In 2025, the last round cutoff for MBA admission at BAMU Aurangabad was 22.6. For the same course, the last round cutoff was 68.29 in 2024, while in 2023, the cutoff was concluded at 54.57.

Maharashtra Common Entrance Test (MAH CET) is a state level management entrance test for admission to MBA/MMS courses. The MAH MBA CET 2025 cutoff has been released for all rounds and categories. Students can view other important dates for MAH MBA CET 2026 in the table below:

Analysing MAH MBA CET cutoff trends provides invaluable insights to students into how admission dynamics and competitiveness for different courses change over time. Students can download year-wise cutoff lists from the table given below to have a better idea of the cutoff percentile changes over the years.

BAMU Aurangabad MAH MCA CET Cutoff 2025 has been released for all rounds and categories for students belonging to the All India and Home State quota. According to the MAH MCA CET Cutoff 2025, the cutoff for MCA course admission ranged from 82.83 to 53.94 for the candidates belonging to the General AI category.
For the General HS category, the cutoff range was 80.54 to 53.94. To know more cutoff information about other categories, rounds, and quotas, see the table below:

As per the MCA Cutoff 2025 for the General category candidates under the Home State quota, the cutoff in the last round of admission was 53.94, whereas, in 2024, admissions were closed at a percentile of 60.05, and in 2023, the last round cutoff was 79.5. This pattern over the years suggests that the competition for MCA admission has decreased over the past 3 years. Students can refer to the table below to know more about other categories and quotas, and rounds year-wise cutoff of 2025, 2024, an
Students aiming for admission to BAMU Aurangabad via the MAH MCA CET for the next academic year can refer to the table below to know the important dates for the MAH MCA CET 2026.

Students who want to gain a seat at BAMU Aurangabad for the MCA course should know the cutoff trends over the years to better understand what percentiles are required for admissions. To help with the same, the table below contains the consolidated year-wise PDFs for 2025, 2024, and 2023.

According to the last round of M.E./M.Tech cutoff 2025 for the General HS category, the top two most competitive specialisations were M.Tech. in Chemical Engineering (Drugs and Pharmaceuticals), followed by M.Tech. in Food Technology, with the cutoff being 87 and 3.33, respectively.
See the table below for more such comparisons about other categories in different rounds and years.

BAMU Aurangabad cutoff 2024 was concluded for Bihar UGEAC exam after a single round of seat allotment. With a Round 1 closing cutoff rank of 5163, B.E. in Electrical Engineering turned out to be the most in-demand B.E specialisation to get into BAMU Aurangabad. Other than that, the Last-Round cutoff rank for B.E. in Civil Engineering stood at 6011 for the General AI quota. For more details regarding the course, rounds, quotas, and category-wise Bihar UGEAC cutoff ranks, students can use the filt

BAMU Aurangabad MPEd cutoff 2025 for the General AI category in the last round was 112.78. In 2024, the cutoff was 27.15. This huge gap in the cutoff scores indicates that the competition for this course in the General AI category has increased from 2024 to 2025.

highlights.description: BAMU Aurangabad has been continuously recognised as one of the leading institutes by numerous national ranking agencies. The institute is ranked #151-200 in the University category, and #51-100 under State Public University by the NIRF 2025. The academic reputation, dedicated faculty, and well-laid infrastructure are some of the reasons of the institute's high ranks among numerous ranking agencies. The article below covers all the information around BAMU Aurangabad rankings:

nirf.description:
BAMU Aurangabad has been ranked well by the NIRF under the 'University' category. Through the years, the NIRF rankings for BAMU Aurangabad indicate the institute's strong performance in crucial areas such as the Quality of Education, Conducting Impactful Research, the Expertise of Faculty members, and Overall Institutional Management. These good rankings not only prove the institute's positive reputation but also highlight its excellence across diverse domains, evaluated against the standards established by the NIRF. It has been seen that in the 'University' category, the institute's ranks have shown a fluctuating trend when evaluated against the ranking of the prior year. In 2025, BAMU Aurangabad fell 50 ranks to #151 rank in the 'University' category by the NIRF ranking.
It has been noticed that in the 'State Public University' category, the institute's ranking has fallen when compared against previous year's ranking. In 2025, BAMU Aurangabad fell 5 ranks to #51 rank in the 'State Public University' category by the NIRF ranking.
Explore the most recent rankings from other ranking bodies and their respective categories below.
Read less
