reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Deogiri CollegeReviewed on 19 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Teachers are not so helpful. The facilities are poor.
reviews.review_text: Infrastructure is average. The facilities are basic and average level. But good too if you don't expect much from colleges, the mess and canteen are also basic. Labs and library are good, though. We can study there, but avoid people. It is more about sports, hence sports are always played.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by Maharashtra Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 18 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: My college is good. You can get a nice faculty to communicate with.
reviews.review_text: In the classroom, there is a projector. The projector will be presented through a laptop and it is very easy to understand the concept and logic through the projector. The facility of Wi-Fi connection is the best for students. In the virtual labs, there are computers and a projector. There is a library too and in that library there are so many types of books. The food quality is so good. Every food item is so tasty to eat.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by Shreeyash College of Engineering and TechnologyReviewed on 15 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: This is the best for study, practical knowledge, and good placement, with really good faculty.
reviews.review_text: Continually clean rooms and bathrooms. I have experience with the hostel. In the hostel, the best room provides hot water at any time in the morning, and there is electricity at any time. The rooms and washrooms are friendly, and the faculty for the hostel follows fairness, not a different approach.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by Shreeyash College of Engineering and TechnologyReviewed on 7 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Good faculty, placement facility not good, infrastructure and hostel facility are perfect.
reviews.review_text: Wi-Fi is available throughout the campus, and there are multiple labs, such as the project lab, the computer lab, and the programming lab. The modern classroom and central library are used for different courses. There is a large campus with a hostel, college, and other administrative buildings. Food quality is good and different in the hostel. Cricket and football grounds are present on the campus.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by Maharashtra Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 3 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Review by a genuine student of MIT CSN.
reviews.review_text: The campus is large, green, and well-equipped with labs, Wi-Fi, a library, sports facilities, and hostels. That's all.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Shreeyash College of Engineering and TechnologyReviewed on 3 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Absolutely satisfying infrastructure and environment.
reviews.review_text: There are a lot of rooms, about 30 for classes of different departments, equipped with air conditioning. There is Wi-Fi in every section of the college with good speed. The college library is so good, it has a wide variety of book collections. We can hire books for a few weeks.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Shreeyash College of Engineering and TechnologyReviewed on 3 Jan 2026)
reviews.review_heading: Abruptly satisfied with the faculty members.
reviews.review_text: There are a lot of rooms for classes of different departments equipped with air-conditioning. There is Wi-Fi in every section of the college with good speed. The college library is so good, it has a wide variety of book collections. We can also hire books for a few weeks.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Shreeyash College of Engineering and TechnologyReviewed on 18 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: The college is good for average students with a CET of 10-50 percent.
reviews.review_text: The college infrastructure, in my opinion, is good. There are network issues in the department because of the underground, but the Wi-Fi facility is available. Labs are available. Classrooms are also good. The library is also good, and all books are available in the library. The facilities like bus, hostel, canteen, and stationery are available in college, and also the beautiful, big green campus. The mess food is average. The medical facilities are also available because there is a B.A.M.S. Hos...

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by Shreeyash College of Engineering and TechnologyReviewed on 7 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Shreeyash College of Engineering review.
reviews.review_text: The facilities and infrastructure available for our course are good enough. Wi-Fi facilities are available. Labs are well maintained and had all the instruments required for practicals. Modern classrooms and computer labs are available. The hostel quality of our college is okay. Basic medical facilities are available.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2026Offered by Shreeyash College of Engineering and TechnologyReviewed on 6 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: A combination of both positive and negative thoughts about college.
reviews.review_text: Within the college, there are 7 to 8 computer laboratories that are highly equipped with the latest software and hardware, maintained at a high cost. reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by Maharashtra Institute of TechnologyReviewed on 4 Dec 2025)
reviews.review_heading: There is a good learning environment.
reviews.review_text: Some students' behaviour in college is bad. The environment is not very clean. Students are not allowed to sit outside. Some classrooms are poorly maintained with broken lights and switchboards, which need improvement. The campus life here is average. My interaction with my classmates is normal for studying.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2025Offered by Y. B. Chavan College of PharmacyReviewed on 24 Nov 2025)
reviews.review_heading: Satisfied with good staff and supportive placement.
reviews.review_text: The classroom was good and is full of facilities. It has a Wi-Fi connection, and smart classrooms are available. The library is really very good, and all departments and their assistants are really very supportive and helpful. I am not a hosteler, but the hostel facility is also good.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by G.S. Mandal's Marathwada Institute of Technology (MIT, Aurangabad)
reviews.review_heading: Our college is good.
reviews.review_text: Our college provides Wi-Fi, good electricity, computer labs, a library for students to study, a mess, and food that is very nice. The college also has a playground for playing various outdoor games. The college must hold various festivals once a year for the entertainment of the students.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2029Offered by G.S. Mandal's Marathwada Institute of Technology (MIT, Aurangabad)
reviews.review_heading: Best college for core fields. Must consider G.S. Mandal's MIT.
reviews.review_text: Best labs, library in the whole city, well-maintained great classrooms, Wi-Fi campus, great hostel for boys and girls. Best food services like mess and cafeteria as well. Various clubs like NCC Sports are also helping to make campus life very good and enjoyable. Great programs like motivation are also held.

reviews.batch: Batch of 2027Offered by Deogiri Institute of Engineering and Management StudiesReviewed on 5 Oct 2025)
reviews.review_heading: My review of Deogiri Institute of Engineering and Management Studies.
reviews.review_text: The CSE and AIML departments are equipped with modern infrastructure and facilities to support academic and practical learning. The department has a well-classroom. The student also has access to the digital library as well as the normal library. Hostel facilities are comfortable, and the girls' hostel is on the campus itself, with a closing time of 7 pm.

reviews_list.batch_info: B.V.Sc. & A.H. - Batch of 2028
reviews_list.review_title: Faculty are well qualified and experienced.

review_content.Placements: The top college in Rajasthan that offers B.V.Sc. and A.H. 100 percent of placements are offered. The average package is 7.5 LPA and above. Semi-government agencies and mobile veterinary vans provide jobs as a veterinary officer. 100 percent of students got internships under the college for 1 year.
review_content.Infrastructure: Great experience overall. Everything is awesome, including cleanliness, atmosphere, food, and the caring hostel warden, who is always there to help. High-speed internet is available in all rooms. The food in the mess is good, and there’s a canteen available for students. There’s also a large sports field.

reviews_list.review_title: Our college is the best out of 3 because it produces topper every year.

review_content.Placements: Almost 85% of students got placed in our course. The highest salary package offered is 1 crore per annum. The lowest salary package offered is 10 LPA. In all years, our college produced toppers in batch.
review_content.Infrastructure: In our college, more facilities are available like Wi-Fi, air-conditioners, and ventilation windows. Labs are very good and special. Many facilities are available in our college that I can't tell you in words.

review_content.Placements: The level of skills taught to us is pathetic because of a very low number of patients and high charges. The efficiency of a student to be hired is hampered overall. Hardly anyone gets high paying jobs after completion, but you can't solely blame the college for it because the state of the profession is in dire straits now anyway.

highlights.summary:
The NEET MDS 2026 application process has commenced for Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre admission to the MDS course, with Mar 30, 2026, being the last date to register. Also, the NEET MDS exam 2026 is going to be held on May 2, 2026.

A few more updates are given below:

faqs.answer: A:    Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre has received an impressive review rating of 3.25 on Placements based on 4 feedback of verified Shiksha students.
faqs.question: Does Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre provide good placement facilities?

fees_and_eligibility.overview: Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre is an institute which offers various courses at UG and PG levels. Students can choose Full Time programmes from stream such as Medicine & Health Sciences. At the UG level, students can choose the available programmes such as BDS. Talking about PG level, some of the available courses include MDS. The fee structure for the Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre programmes is around INR 8,54,460 to 15,69,000.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) - Batch of 2023
reviews_list.review_title: It is one of the decent dental colleges in Chhattisgarh.

review_content.Placements: Since this is a dental college, there are no placements. Passouts are self-employed, by opening the clinic. Some even go for post-graduation in clinical as well as non-clinical branches. Some students join various dental colleges as BDS staff.
review_content.Infrastructure: There are 3 lecture rooms on the campus. There is a well-equipped library with almost all the medical and dental books available in it. Hostels offer double or triple room sharing with common washrooms. There is 1 hostel with an attached washroom, but students have to share that room with 4 students in total. Mess is average, with 3 meals per day. The canteen is non-existent at the moment. There is a badminton court for recreation purposes.

reviews_list.batch_info: Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) - Batch of 2023
reviews_list.review_title: It provides good teachers but the infrastructure is not maintained, and the experience is average.

review_content.Placements: Placements can not be provided in the field of dentistry. Students can either open a private clinic, join an already set and renowned clinic, pursue MS or appear for some competitive exam for a government job. There are various diploma courses also available in various specialties.
review_content.Infrastructure: It provides digital and spacious lecture halls although the furniture is not maintained well. Library and Wi-Fi are provided. The mess facility is not that good. Food is average and almost special meals are there. The campus has no dental store, medical store, or utility shop. Hostel rooms are good but not maintained regularly.

individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) - Batch of 2025
individual_reviews.title: Good infrastructure and faculty, value for money .

content.Placements: Package offered depends entirely on performance, Students can join private dental clinics and hospitals and also appear for NEET MS exam for further studies. Post graduation facility is also available in some departments. Approximately 90% 0f the students get placements.
content.Infrastructure: Classrooms are average. Hostel facility and mess facility are also average. Food quality is good but there's no much variety in menu. individual_reviews.course: Bachelor of Dental Surgery (BDS) - Batch of 2023
individual_reviews.title: Faculty members are quite good and very supportive, and the infrastructure is quite satisfactory.

content.Placements: In BDS and MBBS courses, placements are not available through institutions, and students have to bag it up. Internship programs are available in the nearest hospitals and clinics. About 86% of the students got an internship in the last three years, which will further improve.
content.Infrastructure: Classrooms of our college are well-equipped with projector and cameras. Wi-Fi is available in our college. The library contains ample stock of rare books, and there is also a digital library. Hostel rooms and surroundings are very neat and clean, and they also have Wi-Fi. Food quality is good, and they serve pure vegetarian food. Hostelers are quite active in sports and games.

description: Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre cutoff 2025 is available on this page for various accepting exams. Candidates can refer to the exam-wise sections below to view the cutoff details across different rounds and categories. Other important updates are as follows:

Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre NEET UG cutoff 2025 was concluded with the release of the final seat allotment result for admission to the BDS course. The cutoff was released for several categories belonging to the AI quota. For the General AI quota, the NEET UG last round closing rank was 1087735 for BDS admission. Candidates can refer to the table below to view the first and last round cutoffs 2025 for the General AI category students.

Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre BDS cutoff 2025 was 1087735 for the students belonging to the General AI quota. Similarly, for the OBC category students, the NEET UG last round closing rank was 1044348 for admission to the BDS course. For other categories and rounds, use the provided filters.

Maitri College of Dentistry and Research Centre accepts the NEET UG entrance exam for selecting students to the BDS course. Candidates with a valid score are then eligible for the NEET UG counselling. For 2026 admission, aspirants must refer to the table below to view some of the important NEET UG 2026 dates to be eligible for admission.

Candidates preparing for admission can refer to the table below to view or download the NEET UG round and year-wise consolidated cutoff list. This will help students to understand the past three years' cutoff trends.

This year, in 2025, the cutoff rank for admission to BDS was 4487 for the General AI category students._x000D_ On the other hand, for the OBC category candidates, the Round 2 cutoff rank for the same course stood at 4678, suggesting that the competition for the General AI category is higher as compared to the OBC category. To view the cutoff details category-wise and round-wise for 2025, 2024, and 2023, use the provided filters.

faqs.answer: Some medical field which offe best work and life balance are shown below -Dentistry (BDS & MDS) – Fixed clinic hours, fewer emergencies.Pharmacy (BPharm & MPharm) – Less stress compared to hospital-based jobs.Physiotherapy (BPT & MPT) – Flexible working hours, minimal emergency calls.Public Health & Community Medicine – Focus on preventive healthcare rather than emergency treatments.Clinical Research & Medical Writing – Research-based work with no direct patient responsibilities.
faqs.question: Q: Which Medical fields offer the best work-life balance?

faqs.answer: H. P. Government Dental College And Hospital, Shimla has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses: CoursesEligibilityMDSCandidate must have secured 50 percentile (40 in case of SC/ST/OBC, including PWD of SC/ST/OBC & 45 in case of UR PWD candidates) in NEET- MDS 2023.BDSCandidate must have passed in Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology, and English individually and must have obtained mentioned percentage taken together in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Biotechnology. In respect of PwBD candidates as per NMC and DCI Regulations, the minimum marks in the qualifying examination in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (or Botany & Zoology)/Biotechnology taken together shall be 40% instead of 50%. Candidates who have passed Class 12 from Open School or as private candidates shall not be eligible to appear for theNational Eligibility Cum Entrance Test'. However, this has been a subject matter of challenge before the Hon'ble High Court of Delhi, Hon'ble High Court of Allahabad, Lucknow Bench, and Hon'ble High Court of Madhya Pradesh at Jabalpur The lower age limit is 17 years on or before 31st Dec of the year of admission (born on or before 31.12.2006) Result awaited candidates can also apply.UG DiplomaCandidate must have completed 10+2 from any recognised Board/Council.

faqs.question: What is the eligibility to get admission in H. P. Government Dental College And Hospital, Shimla for UG/PG courses?
faqs.answer:
H. P. Government Dental College And Hospital, Shimla has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses:CoursesEligibilityMDSCandidate must have secured 50 percentile (40 in case of SC/ST/OBC, including PWD of SC/ST/OBC & 45 in case of UR PWD candidates) in NEET- MDS 2023.BDSCandidate must have passed in Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology, and English individually and must have obtained mentioned percentage taken together in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Biotechnology. In respect of PwBD candidates as per NMC and DCI Regulations, the minimum marks in the qualifying examination in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (or Botany & Zoology)/Biotechnology taken together shall be 40% instead of 50%.
